property.name: Four Seasons Hotel Buenos Aires
property.description: Set in an elegant tower and a belle epoque mansion, this luxury hotel facing Plaza Soldi is 5 minutes' walk from high-end shops at Patio Bullrich, and 32 km from Ministro Pistarini International Airport. Plush rooms offer free Wi-Fi and flat-screens, plus minibars and marble bathrooms; some have access to a private lounge. Suites add sitting areas or living rooms; upgraded suites include free airport transfers and/or 24-hour butlers. Room service is available 24/7. There are 2 posh restaurants, including an open-air eatery with a fire pit, as well as a lively bar. Other amenities include a spa and a heated outdoor pool, plus a sauna and a gym.
property.address: Posadas 1086 88, C1011ABB Cdad. Autónoma de Buenos Aires, Argentina

nearby_places.name: Recoleta Cemetery
nearby_places.description: Built in 1822, this statue-rich cemetery features mausoleums of many notables, including Eva Perón.

nearby_places.name: Teatro Colón
nearby_places.description: This grand theater (circa 1908) known for acoustics hosts classical music, operas, ballets & tours.

nearby_places.name: El Ateneo Grand Splendid
nearby_places.description: This converted theater was first opened in 1919 & has now been converted into an elegant book store.

nearby_places.name: Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes
nearby_places.description: One of Latin America's largest public art collections, set in a converted drainage pumping station.

nearby_places.name: Plaza de Mayo
nearby_places.description: City square named after 1810 revolution that took place here, leading to Argentina's independence.

nearby_places.name: Museo de Arte Latinoamericano de Buenos Aires
nearby_places.description: Modern museum showcasing 20th century & newer Latin American artwork, cultural events & films.

nearby_places.name: Casa Rosada
nearby_places.description: Venerable, pink-colored palace that houses the presidential office & features guided tours.

nearby_places.name: Obelisco
nearby_places.description: Unveiled in 1936, this landmark obelisk made from Cordoba white stone is a popular meeting place.

nearby_places.name: Palacio Barolo
nearby_places.description: Distinctive landmark tower block with layout & design features inspired by Dante's "Divine Comedy."

nearby_places.name: Floralis Generica
nearby_places.description: A city gift from an architect, this striking steel flower sculpture sits above a reflecting pool.

nearby_places.name: Puente de la Mujer
nearby_places.description: The 160m-long pedestrian suspension swing bridge was designed by architect Santiago Calatrava.

nearby_places.name: Buenos Aires Metropolitan Cathedral
nearby_places.description: The former seat of Pope Francis, this cathedral has a history dating back to the early 17th century.

nearby_places.name: Reserva Ecológica Costanera Sur
nearby_places.description: Waterside city nature reserve with lagoons, marshland & forest, plus walking & cycling trails.

nearby_places.name: Centro Cultural Recoleta
nearby_places.description: The 1732 building, one of the city's oldest, is a cultural center with concerts, classes & exhibits.

rooms.name: Twin Room with City View
rooms.description: 1 king bed, 1 twin bed · Breakfast included

on_other_sites.text: We had an absolutely wonderful stay at the Four Seasons Hotel Buenos Aires and cannot recommend it highly enough!||From the moment we arrived, the entire team made us feel genuinely welcome. Every single staff member was warm, attentive, and always ready to help — no matter the time of day. Their hospitality truly made a difference and set the tone for our entire stay.||We were delighted to receive a room upgrade upon check-in, which was a lovely and unexpected surprise. The room itself was beautifully appointed, very spacious and comfortable, and housekeeping was impeccable throughout our stay.||Breakfast was a real highlight — varied, fresh, and delicious every morning. And in the evenings, the hotel bar is the perfect place to unwind with a glass of champagne after a day of exploring this magnificent city.||The location is also excellent — well situated and convenient for getting around Buenos Aires.||Overall, our stay was simply perfect. We will definitely be back!
It was a wonderful stay in Four Seasons Hotel Buenos Aires. The price is very expensive but the service and hospitality is really amaze. Can consider good value for money
